participants.reasoning: The extracted participants match the official description of 100 community volunteers with knee osteoarthritis meeting ACR criteria.
participants.extracted: One hundred adults with knee osteoarthritis were randomized and allocated to two groups: the Exercise Group (n=50) and the Orientation Group (n=50). Eligibility criteria were age 50 to 75 years and a diagnosis of knee OA according to American College of Rheumatology criteria, with radiographic confirmation (Kellgren-Lawrence grade 2 or higher) and a recent knee x-ray within the past 12 months. Exclusion criteria included fibromyalgia, unstable heart condition, physical activity more than twice per week, inability to pedal a stationary bicycle, previous knee arthroplasty, and occurrence of adverse events. The analyzed sample comprised 81 patients who completed the study (43 in the Exercise Group and 38 in the Orientation Group).
participants.official:
Knee OA recruitment
100 community volunteers
ACR criteria

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official intervention details: an in-person supervised strengthening program over 8 weeks with twice-weekly sessions and an attention-control group receiving educational material plus reinforcing phone calls.
interventions.extracted: Participants were allocated to two groups. The exercise group received an 8-week quadriceps strengthening program consisting of twice-weekly group sessions lasting 30 to 40 minutes, including 10 minutes of stationary bicycle warm-up, ischiotibial stretching, and three sets of 15 seated knee extension repetitions at approximately 50–60% of the load estimated from a 10-repetition maximum test, with resistance increased according to tolerance. This group also received an educational orientation manual. The orientation group received the same educational manual with explanations about knee osteoarthritis and advice on managing functional difficulties, plus two reinforcing phone calls over the 8 weeks, but no supervised exercise.
interventions.official:
Clinic, supervised group setting, delivered entirely in‐person
1. Attention control; biweekly phone calls to encourage health education; 3 synchronous contact occasions
2. Exercise (strengthening): 16 sessions (2 x 30 to 40 minutes per week over 8 weeks); 16 synchronous contact occasions

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes include TUG and multiple SF-36 quality-of-life subscales, but the official outcomes only report overall pain and SF-36 functional capacity with no quality-of-life measure.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure pain intensity using the Numerical Rating Scale (NRS, 0–10), functional performance using the Timed Up and Go test (TUG, time in seconds to complete the chair rise-walk-turn-sit task), and quality of life using the SF-36 questionnaire (subscales including functional capacity, physical aspects, pain, general health, vitality, social aspects, emotional aspects, and mental health, scored 0–100). These outcomes were assessed pre-intervention and post-intervention at the end of the 8-week treatment period.
outcomes.official:
At 8 weeks (immediately post‐intervention)
Pain (global/overall pain VAS/NRS)
Physical function (SF‐36 functional capacity)
No quality of life measure
No participant‐reported treatment success meaure
